name: gersemi_cmake
description: CMake language grammar from the gersemi formatter (the lark grammar its rust-backend is tested against). Upstream also passes an inline transformer; the bank drops it and freezes the raw tree.
alt_grammar_finding: No tree-identical workaround edit is known. Dropping UNQUOTED_ELEMENT's (?!\[=*\[) guards changes Python Lark's own trees on 3/8 inputs (verified 2026-06-12). Hoisting a single leading guard onto UNQUOTED_ARGUMENT (which lark-rs can build via the unbounded-leading-lookahead lowering) is corpus-identical on 8/8 AND lark-rs-on-edit == Python-on-original, but is provably divergent on inputs like `$[=[x]=]` (internal element boundaries after $/escape/reference no longer re-check the guard), so it does not qualify: an alt grammar must be tree-identical by construction, not by corpus coincidence.
